citations.narrative:
The facility is not following the written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC):
-The PVC states to administer Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines yearly for adult dogs. Adult male Shih Tzu (#6450),
Adult male Bichon (#4318), Adult male Cavalier King Charles Spaniel (#7507), Adult male Poodle (#3271), and Adult
female Yorkshire Terrier (#0705) last received these vaccines in 1/1/2025.
-The PVC states that 10% of litters and 10% of adults will be fecal tested yearly. Fecal sampling was not performed in
2025.
Failure to administer required vaccines and conduct sampling in accordance with a veterinary approved schedule may
result in animals to not be adequately protected against disease and/or can cause pain and distress. Correct by ensuring
all animals are vaccinated and sampled in accordance with the schedule approved by the Attending Veterinarian and
contact the Attending Veterinarian to review records and create a plan to determine when to administer future
vaccinations for the applicable animals. Correct by 14-APRIL-2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
n

narrative:
3.13(a)(3) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility is not following the written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC): 
 
-The PVC states to administer Distemper and Parvovirus vaccines yearly for adult dogs. Adult male Shih Tzu (#6450), 
Adult male Bichon (#4318), Adult male Cavalier King Charles Spaniel (#7507), Adult male Poodle (#3271), and Adult 
female Yorkshire Terrier (#0705) last received these vaccines in 1/1/2025. 
 
-The PVC states that 10% of litters and 10% of adults will be fecal tested yearly. Fecal sampling was not performed in 
2025. 
 
Failure to administer required vaccines and conduct sampling in accordance with a veterinary approved schedule may 
result in animals to not be adequately protected against disease and/or can cause pain and distress. Correct by ensuring 
all animals are vaccinated and sampled in accordance with the schedule approved by the Attending Veterinarian and 
contact the Attending Veterinarian to review records and create a plan to determine when to administer future 
vaccinations for the applicable animals. Correct by 14-APRIL-2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a Facility Representative.
